research.title: DemoPSD: Disagreement-Modulated Policy Self-Distillation
research.rawTextSnippet: On-policy self-distillation (OPSD) has emerged as a practical method for training large language models (LLMs) to reason, where a single model acts as both the teacher and the student with different levels of information access. However, recent studies have found that the teacher's dense token-level…

research.rawTextSnippet: As AI coding agents become more autonomous, they increasingly ship code iteratively, with the codebase persisting across sessions. research.rawTextSnippet: LLMs memorize sensitive training data, including personally identifiable information (PII), creating a pressing need for reliable post hoc removal methods. dailyBrief: Today's AI landscape reveals significant challenges and innovations. Reports indicate that GPT-5.5's Codex may be experiencing degraded performance due to reasoning-token clustering, raising concerns for businesses relying on this technology for critical applications. Additionally, potential session and cache leakage between workspace instances could pose security risks, prompting companies to reassess their data protection strategies. On a positive note, the introduction of Mouse, a precision editing tool for AI coding agents, promises to enhance coding efficiency, potentially reducing development time. However, Ford's decision to rehire human engineers after AI failed to meet quality standards underscores the ongoing need for human oversight in AI-driven processes, highlighting a critical balance between automation and quality assurance in manufacturing.
